title: Pedilonum rupestre

intro: Pedilonum rupestre, also known as the rocky Pedilanthus, is a succulent plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pedilonum rupestre include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
